sutta: once the exalted 1 was staying near savarti and jeter grove in anatap indiga's park then a great number of monks robin themselves in the morning and taking bowl and rope set out for savathi questing for arms food now it occurred to those monsters it is too early yet to arrange savati for arms food suppose we visit the park of the wanderers holding other views accordingly they did so and on getting there greeted them courteously and after after the exchange of greetings and reminiscent talk sat down at 1 side so seated those wonders of other views said this to them your references gotama the recluse thus teaches dhamma come you monks do you thoroughly grasp all dhamma do you dwell ever and always thoroughly grasping all dhamma now your references we also thus teach dhamma to our followers come your references do you thoroughly grasp all dhamma do you ever and always dwell thoroughly grasping all dharma hearing your references pray or please what is the distinction what is the specific feature what is the difference between gotama the recluse and ourselves that is between his teaching of dhamma and ours all his way of instruction and ours thereupon those monks made no reply either of approval or of disapproval to those wondrous holding other views but without expressing either approval or disapproval they rose up and went away saying we will learn the meaning of this saying in the company of the exalted 1 so those monks after arranging savati for arms food on returning from their arms round and after eating their meal went to see the exalted 1 and on coming to him saluted him and sat down at 1 side saluted here means being homage so seated they said this to the exalted 1 sir here in the morning we robe ourselves etc etc and they related the entire incident and how they went away without answering the wonders and the buddha said monks when wanderers holding other views speak thus they should be spoken to thus the 1 question the 1 statement the 1 explanation the 2 questions the 2 statements the 2 explanations the 3 the 4 the 5 etc up to the 10 questions the 10 statements the 10 explanations thus question monks the wondrous holding other views will fail to answer and further will come to discomfiture or uncomfortableness why so because monks that is beyond their scope monks i behold not in the world with this devas its maras its brahmas its host of recluses and brahmanas together with his devas and mankind i behold not 1 who could convince the mind with an explanation to these questions save only the wayfarer or 1 of his disciples or 1 who has heard it from that source and just stop a moment the way pharaoh must be referring the translation for the tata gata as now we also read the word tata gatta is 1 of the names of the buddha and then the buddha continue as to the saying the 1 question the 1 statement the 1 explanation owing to what was it said monks if in 1 thing among rightly feels revulsion rightly feels fading interests in the world rightly be released rightly have sight to the furthest bounds and rightly comprehend the meaning of things then in this same visible state of same lifetime he makes an end of ill or dukkha in what 1 thing in this namely all beings are persistent by food or all beings subsist by food or new treatment in this 1 thing monks if a monk rightly feels revulsion rightly feel fading interest etc he makes an end of ill the 1 questioned the 1 statement the 1 explanation was said because of this as to the saying the 2 questions the 2 statements the 2 explanations owing to what was it said monks if in 2 things among rightly feel revulsion rightly feel fading interest etc then in this same visible state he makes an end of ill in what 2 things in mentality materiality nama rupa in these 2 things if a man rightly feel revulsion he makes an end of ill the 2 questions the 2 statements the 2 explanations were said because of this as to the saying the 3 questions the 3 statements the 3 explanations going to what was it said monks if in 3 things among rightly feel revulsion feel fading interest etc he makes an end of ill in what 3 things the 3 feelings vedana in these 3 things if a man rightly feel revulsion etc he makes an end of ill as as to the saying the 4 questions the 4 statements the 4 explanations going to what was it said monks if in 4 things among rightly fear revulsion etc he makes an end of ill in what 4 things the 4 nutrients are sustenance sustenances of food ahara even these 4 things among rightly fear revulsion he makes an end of you as to the saying the 5 questions the 5 statements the 5 explanations going to what was it said monks even 5 things among rightly fear revulsion etc it makes an end of ill what 5 things the 5 aggregates of attachment if in these 5 things among rightly feel revulsion he makes an end of ill as to the saying the 6 questions the 6 statements the 6 explanations going to what was it said monks even 6 things among rightly feel revulsion etc he makes an end of ill what 6 things the 6 fears in the cell that means the 6 internal bases even these 6 things among rightly feels revulsion he makes an end of you as to the saying the 7 questions are 7 statements or 7 explanations going to what was it said monks if in 7 things among rightly fear revulsion etc he makes an end of ill what 7 things the 7 stations of consciousness as to the saying there's 8 questions the 8 statements the 8 explanations going to what was it said monks even 8 things among rightly feel revulsion etc it makes an end of ill what 8 things the 8 worldly conditions as to the saying the 9 questions the 9 statements the 9 explanations owing to what was it said monks if in 9 things among rightly field revulsion etc he makes an end of ill what 9 things the 9 abodes of beings as to the saying the 10 questions the 10 statements the 10 explanations owing to what was it said monks if in 10 things among rightly feel revulsion rightly feel fading interest in the world rightly be released rightly have sight to the furthest bounds and rightly comprehend the meaning of things then in the same visible state he makes an end of ill what 10 things the 10 wrong ways of action or kama and that's the end of the suta
commentary:
so here in this utah you find sometimes like in here these external aesthetics they they think that they're teaching and the buddha's teaching is basically the same so [Music] when they ask these uh buddha's disciples the buddha's disciples could not answer them but the buddha said we should ask them these 10 things if you ask them these 10 things and they surely will not be able to explain it so what are these 10 things the buddha said now if a manga feels revulsion feels fading interest in these 10 things then he will be released from dukkhala the first 1 is all being subsist by food or nutrient so if a person understands that we continue to live because of food then excuse me then he will start to uh feel not so have such great passion for greed for food so here is interesting that all being subsist by food because some people think once you become a heavenly being you don't need any food it's not true all beings need some kind of food after slater and talk about 4 things you will find what are these what what this food refers to the 2 the 2 things 2 things is mentality materiality nama rupa nama rupa it is said in the suttas that nama rupa arises together with consciousness whenever consciousness arises nama rupa must arise and when consciousness ceases nama rupa also ceases so nama rupa is actually the object of consciousness so being the object of consciousness it is called phenomena phenomena whatever consciousness is conscious of and there are 2 parts here 1 is the mandrel part which is called mentality the other is the physical part which is called materiality so everything in this world our external world and our internal world consists of these 2 things like the mental part and the material part so basically it is the whole world so if you are tired of the world you are tired of mentality and materiality and the third 1 uh refers to the 3 feelings the free feelings the buddha said all feelings are dukkha even sukkah feelings are dukkha in a way in a in a sense in what sense because it is impermanent because whatever sukha feelings we have we tend to cling to it happy feelings or pleasant feelings we tend to cling to it but because it is impermanent it is a source of duka it will fail and you cannot hold on to it so it is also a source of dukkha and of course the other 2 feelings are duka itself unpleasant feelings and the third 1 is uh neither sukkah nor dukkha neither pleasant or unpleasant feelings so all these 3 feelings unsatisfactory so 4 things 4 things here refers to the 4 nutrients ahara and the 4 nutrients that uh beings subsist on the first 1 is solid food like we take solid food and the second 1 is contact because of contact then you have feeling you have perception etc so contact is is considered a new treatment a source of nutrient uh a kind of nutrient the third 1 is mental intention or volition for example the will to live that is a kind of a new treatment then the fourth 1 is consciousness consciousness is another kind of nutrient so the 5 things refers to the 5 aggregates of attachment 5 aggregates of attachment refers to the body feeling perception violation and consciousness these 5 things we take these 5 aggregates we take either to be the cell or as belonging to the cell or as being in the cell or the self as being in the 5 aggregates so whenever we refer to ourselves we always refer to the 5 aggregates so if a person is feels revulsion for these 5 aggregates and then slowly he will make an end of illness he will be liberated the 6 things refers to the 6 internal bases 6 internal bases the eye ear nose tongue body and mind if you feel revulsion for these 6 internal bases of the body and mind then you will also make an end of illness become liberated the 7 things refers to the 7 stations of consciousness uh what are these 7 stations of consciousness these are the 7 stations of consciousness now where beings can exist the first 1 is uh is is where beings have are similar in in body and similar in perception another 1 is another type is the beings are similar in body and different in perception another another type is where the beings are different in body and different in perception then the 1 is where the beings are different in body and same similar in perception so that makes for 4 then the fifth 1 is the base of boundless space then the 6 is the base of boundless consciousness the 7 is the base of nothingness so these are the 7 stations of consciousness where beings exist so if you become tired of these things slowly you will make an end of ill the 8 things refers to the 8 worldly conditions that means pleasure and pain gain and loss uh fame and ill repute and then praise and blame these are the 8 worldly conditions are that move beings now we want 1 and we we don't want the second type for example we want gain and we don't want loss we want pleasure we don't want pain we want to be famous we don't want to have a bad reputation we want to be praised we don't want to be blamed so if you are tired of all these worldly conditions if you revulsion for it slowly you are on your way out of samsara the 9 things refers to the 9 abodes of beings just now we heard the 7 stations of consciousness plus 2 other abodes of beings is the sphere of neither perception or non-perception and the asanya satya asanya satta refers to those beings and without perception if a being does not have perception basically he has he has also no consciousness this is the type of being in the fourth jhana plane so these are the 9 abodes of beings if you're tired of it also you're on your way out of samsara then the last 1 is the 10 wrong ways of action or kama that means the 3 wrong ways of body karma the 4 wrong ways of verbal karma and the 3 wrong ways of mental karma what are the 3 wrong body karmas or evil body karma killing stealing committing adultery these are the 3 wrong body karmas the 4 verbal 1 is lying [Music] carrying tails so that as as to make people quarrel and fight them that means you hear somebody say talk bad about some person and you repeat it elsewhere and you cause people to quarrel and fight then the third 1 is call speech or rough speech and then the fourth 1 is gossip or unbeneficial talk pointless talk and then the 3 mental evil karma is covetousness excessive greed excessive hatred and having wrong view so these are the 10 wrong karma so uh if a person feels revulsion for these things and described then he's on his way out of samsara out of the round of the birds
